title: Peppered Filipino Shrimp Spring-Rolls
description: Peppery, tender shrimp spring rolls infused with clotted cream and dashi, packed with energy and low-carb friendliness.
instructions: Peel and devein 200g shrimp, then simmer in 1 tsp dashi extract, 1 tsp soy sauce, 1 tsp vinegar, and cracked black pepper for 3 minutes.
Drain shrimp and let cool, then mix with 2 tbsp clotted cream until well coated.
Place a thin layer of the shrimp mixture in the center of each lumpia wrapper.
Add a few julienned carrots and sliced red bell pepper for crunch and color.
Roll wrappers tightly, sealing the edges with a dab of water.
Heat 2 tbsp oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Fry the spring rolls until golden brown on all sides, about 3 minutes per side.
Remove from skillet and drain on paper towels.
Slice each roll in half for a better bite.
Serve with a dipping sauce of soy sauce, lime juice, and a pinch of pepper.
